title: Threat Advisory: Credential Stuffing Against Customer Login
text: Analysts observed distributed credential-stuffing attempts against the customer login endpoint, characterized by high-volume authentication failures from rotating residential proxy addresses using previously breached username and password pairs. Recommended defenses include rate limiting per account and per network, mandatory multi-factor authentication, breached-password screening at registration and reset, and anomaly alerts on spikes in failed logins. This advisory is defensive guidance for protecting your own authentication surface.
